text: It is acknowledged unanimously that universal human values provide a foundation for the shaping of a nation and for achieving a nation that is just, peaceful and prosperous. With the process of globalization and modernization taking place today, it is very important for children to learn universal human values early in life. One of the aims of education in Indonesia is the implementation of Pancasila Education in schools, with the purpose of forming individuals that embrace universal human values. A variety of studies have confirmed that universal human values are widely included in Hindu Philosophy. Therefore, this study wishes to explore the possibilities of applying universal human values, based on the elements of Hindu Philosophy, namely with Ahimsa (non-violence), Tat Tvam Asi (Man is One), and Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam (we are one global human family) in order to enrich values-based learning through Pancasila Education in Schools. Research data reveal that Indonesian students have the potential to evolve into citizens with moral values, a personality that upholds justice and an awareness of global citizenship after adopting the universal human values from Hindu Philosophy within the implementation of Pancasila Education in Schools. It has been found that there are several challenges which need to be reviewed by academics, namely teachers’ capacities who will implement the policy and the provision of learning materials, hence there will be many more opportunities that enhance the implementation of the nation’s Philosophy for the building of children who form the nation’s future as it strives to achieve social and political development with high ethical values for a civil society and strong democratic systems of governance.
